Class WaterConductivityMethod

java.lang.Object
neqsim.physicalproperties.methods.PhysicalPropertyMethod
neqsim.physicalproperties.methods.commonphasephysicalproperties.CommonPhysicalPropertyMethod
neqsim.physicalproperties.methods.commonphasephysicalproperties.conductivity.Conductivity
neqsim.physicalproperties.methods.commonphasephysicalproperties.conductivity.WaterConductivityMethod
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, Cloneable, ConductivityInterface, PhysicalPropertyMethodInterface, ThermodynamicConstantsInterface
public class WaterConductivityMethod extends Conductivity
Thermal conductivity of pure water and steam using polynomial correlations fitted to NIST WebBook reference data.

Uses separate correlations for the dilute gas (steam) and liquid regions, with a density-based crossover for the transition/supercritical region.

Dilute gas (steam): quadratic polynomial in T fitted to NIST data 373-1200 K, accuracy better than 2%.

Liquid water: quadratic polynomial in T fitted to NIST data 273-450 K, accuracy better than 2%.

References:

  • NIST WebBook - Thermophysical Properties of Fluid Systems
  • Huber, M.L., Perkins, R.A., et al. (2012). New International Formulation for the Thermal Conductivity of H2O. J. Phys. Chem. Ref. Data 41(3), 033102.
Version:
1.0
Author:
Even Solbraa

  • Field Details

    • serialVersionUID

      private static final long serialVersionUID
      Serialization version UID.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • RHO_GAS_LIMIT

      private static final double RHO_GAS_LIMIT
      Density threshold for gas-like behavior [kg/m3]. Below this density, the steam polynomial is used.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • RHO_LIQ_LIMIT

      private static final double RHO_LIQ_LIMIT
      Density threshold for liquid-like behavior [kg/m3]. Above this density, the liquid polynomial is used.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • STEAM_A0

      private static final double STEAM_A0
      Steam (dilute gas) thermal conductivity coefficients. lambda_steam = A0 + A1*T + A2*T^2 [W/(m*K)]. Fitted to NIST data for steam at 1 bar, 373-1200 K. Accuracy: better than 2%.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

      private static final double STEAM_A1
      See Also:

    • LIQ_B0

      private static final double LIQ_B0
      Liquid water thermal conductivity coefficients. lambda_liq = B0 + B1*T + B2*T^2 [W/(m*K)]. Fitted to NIST data for liquid water at 1 atm, 273-450 K. Accuracy: better than 2%.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • calcConductivity

      public double calcConductivity()

      calcConductivity.

      Returns:
      a double

    • calcSteamConductivity

      private double calcSteamConductivity(double temp)
      Calculates thermal conductivity of steam (dilute water vapor).

      Polynomial in temperature fitted to NIST data. lambda = A0 + A1*T + A2*T^2 [W/(m*K)].

      Parameters:
      temp - temperature in K
      Returns:
      thermal conductivity in W/(m*K)

    • calcLiquidConductivity

      private double calcLiquidConductivity(double temp)
      Calculates thermal conductivity of liquid water.

      Polynomial in temperature fitted to NIST data. lambda = B0 + B1*T + B2*T^2 [W/(m*K)].

      Parameters:
      temp - temperature in K
      Returns:
      thermal conductivity in W/(m*K)
